summary: Classify persistent-history as coordinated, claude-md-only as uncoordinated for CoordinationLift analysis
context: Expanding COORDINATED_CONDITIONS to cover all 8 conditions required deciding the boundary. persistent-history shares conversation context between agents (implicit coordination). claude-md-only provides project conventions but no inter-agent state sharing.
rationale: The benchmark measures whether coordination mechanisms improve multi-agent outcomes. persistent-history actively passes context between agents (coordination). claude-md-only is static project config that doesn't change between sessions (not coordination). file-reload-generic was also missing and clearly coordinated.

alternatives.option: Classify claude-md-only as coordinated since it provides shared conventions
alternatives.reason_rejected: CLAUDE.md is static project config, not inter-agent coordination. It doesn't carry state between sessions.

alternatives.option: Classify persistent-history as uncoordinated since it has no explicit coordination tools
alternatives.reason_rejected: It passes full conversation history between agents — this is active inter-agent context sharing, the definition of coordination.
